coverage_contract.problem_pattern: Determine whether one referenced object is spatially left/right or above/below another object in a generic scene image.
supported_variations: left vs right relation between two named objects
above vs below relation between two named objects
unsupported_variations: depth, distance, overlap, containment, or directional motion relations not reducible to 2D relative position between two identifiable objects
forbidden_case_specific_assumptions: do not hardcode object locations for any representative image
do not use fixed pixel thresholds or numeric coordinates tied to one scene
do not assume the target objects always appear in canonical positions or sizes
do not answer from choice ordering or prompt priors without image grounding
coverage_contract.why_this_should_generalize: The family repeatedly asks the same low-level visual reasoning operation: identify two referenced objects and compare their 2D positions. A reusable visual focus tool plus lightweight decision skill can cover both left/right and above/below across varied scenes without case-specific logic.

coverage_contract.problem_pattern: Determine the relative spatial position of one depicted object with respect to another in generic image scenes, typically answering left/right or above/below multiple-choice questions.
supported_variations: left vs right relation between two identified objects
above vs below relation between two identified objects across varied natural or illustrated scenes
unsupported_variations: depth, occlusion, or front/behind judgments requiring 3D scene inference
forbidden_case_specific_assumptions: do not hardcode any specific object pair such as bell-clock or seesaw-vehicle
do not assume fixed image layouts, canonical object locations, or one-off coordinates from representative cases
do not rely on dataset-specific answer priors like vehicles usually being below playground objects
coverage_contract.why_this_should_generalize: The family uses a stable question form and repeatedly needs focused localization of two referenced objects before comparing their positions, so a reusable generic visual focus primitive should improve performance across many cases.

coverage_contract.problem_pattern: Multiple-choice visual attribute questions that ask for the color of a specific object, where success depends on isolating the referenced object and inspecting its local color rather than reasoning globally.
supported_variations: Color identification for a named foreground object in a natural image
Color lookup when the target object is small or requires localized visual focus
unsupported_variations: Questions requiring object detection plus relational reasoning, counting, reading text, or non-color attributes
forbidden_case_specific_assumptions: Do not hardcode handbag, swim ring, or any specific object category
Do not assume fixed image regions, centers, or one-off coordinates for the target object
Do not rely on answer-choice priors such as common clothing or accessory colors
coverage_contract.why_this_should_generalize: The failures are concentrated in a stable, high-toolability subproblem: localized color extraction for a referenced object. A reusable focus-and-inspect tool can support many direct-attribute cases across the family without depending on case-specific content.
